scope: packages/ui boundary audit with packages/app, packages/app-core, Electrobun, and packages/agent source inspection
principle: packages/ui renders state; plugins provide meaning; Remotes execute desktop/system capabilities; Electrobun brokers shell/runtime boundaries; packages/app composes the production product.

expertDecision.nextAction: plugin-coding-tools edit/search/list parity before reusable trace rendering components
expertDecision.rationale: Trace rendering is a reusable renderer extraction. The higher-priority architecture risk is execution drift because plugin-coding-tools already routes FILE read/write, SHELL, and WORKTREE through the capability router while edit/search/list still own direct local filesystem mechanics.
reviewOrder: Decide list parity against eliza.fs list.
Decide grep/glob/search parity against eliza.fs search or add explicit Remote methods.
Decide edit parity against an explicit edit/patch Remote method.
Add reusable trace rendering components to packages/ui.

findings: packages/app remains the production composition root.
packages/app-core/src/runtime/desktop owns Electrobun shell renderer roots that should stay thin and compose packages/ui.
packages/agent has view registry/routes and UI vocabulary but no React renderer surface.
Plugin UI dependencies and view manifests are legitimate app/plugin surfaces, not hidden Remotes.
packages/ui/src/services/local-inference is the largest wrong-layer risk because it contains Node fs, downloader, device bridge, and LocalInferenceService implementation code.
Browser media helpers in packages/ui are acceptable browser adapters but must not own host voice semantics.
ToolCallEventLog and trajectory components are good seeds for later trace renderer extraction.

entries.notes: The package correctly describes shared primitives, composites, and layout utilities, but the root barrel exports app shell, bridge, desktop bridge, voice, model, and page surfaces. Keep as the rendering package, but tighten execution boundaries over time.
Shared buttons, cards, forms, dialogs, icons, badges, tables, and similar components are the correct core of packages/ui.
Layout shells and workspace chrome are reusable for production app surfaces and future dynamic view frames. They should receive clients/state via props.

This is the large production routing shell. It is currently exported from packages/ui and mounted by packages/app. That is a real hidden app boundary; do not move it in this phase, but it is not a reusable design-system primitive.

Chat, settings, apps, browser workspace, documents, memory, runtime, logs, database, skills, tasks, and training route views are product surfaces. Extract reusable renderers later, but keep the current route ownership stable until maintainers approve a split.
DynamicViewLoader is a renderer and interaction adapter for remote view bundles. It should not own Electrobun registry/session state, but it is a valid reusable renderer.
This is already a pure renderer for tool-call event payloads and is a strong seed for trace timelines and agent-run dynamic views.
Voice status, pill, settings, emotion, player, and recorder renderers can stay. They should render or collect browser media only and must not own the host voice pipeline.

Browser microphone capture and audio playback helpers are UI/browser concerns, but they overlap with eliza.voice host instrumentation. Keep as browser adapters only; do not let these own VAD, ASR, TTS, or host playback policy.

Model hub cards, routing matrix, downloads, hardware badges, and status panels are reusable renderers for packages/app and dynamic views. They should consume typed snapshots from app-core, eliza.local-model, or plugin-local-inference APIs.

This directory carries a documented UI-side mirror, but it also contains Node fs, downloader, device bridge, and LocalInferenceService implementation code. The rendering package should keep only catalog/type/pure helpers or re-exports from shared; execution should stay in app-core, plugin-local-inference, or eliza.local-model.
Typed HTTP/WebSocket clients are useful bridge interfaces. Keep them as clients over runtime/local APIs; avoid slipping direct host execution into UI clients.
Renderer-safe Electrobun RPC wrappers are acceptable as bridge adapters. They should stay thin and typed; capability execution remains behind Electrobun/app-core and Remotes.
This client uses Electrobun renderer RPC first and falls back to runtime HTTP. That is a reasonable bridge pattern, but future eliza.computer scope should keep this interface thin.
Platform detection, window shell routing, storage bridge, and desktop workspace clients are UI adapters. Keep them as thin adapters over injected clients, Capacitor, or Electrobun bridge calls.
Avatar host and presets are rendering widgets. They may consume voice/emotion state but should not own voice model execution.
App/plugin surface registration and overlay app renderers are close to the dynamic-view direction. Keep rendering here, but plugin meaning and execution stay outside packages/ui.
Character editor and hub are product surfaces with reusable subcomponents. Keep stable for now; later split pure editor components from app navigation and persistence assumptions.
Onboarding is product flow state plus shared components. Keep until the app/ui split is explicit; do not let it control runtime lifecycle directly.
Terminal palette/theme belongs in the rendering kit. PTY session creation and command execution must stay in eliza.pty through the runtime broker.
packages/app is the production composition layer. It imports @elizaos/ui, app-core desktop runtimes, Capacitor bridges, and app plugins. It should remain the product shell.
This is an app-owned overlay that renders the shared VoicePill. It should not own the voice pipeline.

app-core hides real renderer roots for detached desktop windows and tray behavior. This is acceptable Electrobun/app-core shell code, but it must remain shell composition over @elizaos/ui components and not become another product UI fork.
app-core owns mobile/runtime platform bridges, including limited DOM fallback paths. That is not hidden shared UI; it is platform bootstrap and should stay outside packages/ui.
Electrobun correctly owns dynamic view manifests, registry, sessions, open/close/push RPC, and host placement. packages/ui should render view components, not own this registry.

TraceStore and TraceService belong in Electrobun/app-core. The current agent.run.trace HTML asset is a dynamic view implementation; reusable trace timeline rendering could later move into packages/ui.
VoiceService, live adapter, validation harness, budgets, and stream coordination belong outside packages/ui. packages/ui can render snapshots, marks, and budget results.
packages/agent serves plugin-declared view metadata, bundles, search, navigation, and interact requests. That is agent/runtime view infrastructure, not UI rendering.
The agent owns an LLM-facing UI spec/component catalog. It is not React UI, but it is hidden UI vocabulary. Keep it aligned with packages/ui primitives and dynamic-view capabilities.
The standalone agent serves built React dashboard assets in production mode. This is deployment/runtime hosting, not a shared UI component.
app-core mirrors UI route/settings descriptors for API/dev routes. These should remain typed contracts or generated metadata, not drift-prone hand copies.

traceComponentCandidates: future packages/ui trace timeline for TraceEvent lists
future packages/ui latency budget pass/fail badges
future packages/ui model/tool/capability event grouping components
future migration target for reusable pieces of packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/trace/views/agent-run-trace.html
runtimeDesktopCouplingRisks: packages/ui/src/services/local-inference contains Node fs/downloader/device bridge implementation in the rendering package
packages/ui/src/App.tsx is a product shell exported from the shared UI package
packages/ui/src/components/pages contains production route views and not just reusable renderers
packages/ui/src/bridge/electrobun-rpc exposes concrete Electrobun bridge wrappers from the UI package
packages/ui/src/voice/local-asr-capture.ts and audio hooks own browser mic/playback helpers that overlap with host voice semantics
packages/app-core/src/runtime/desktop owns hidden renderer roots outside packages/app
packages/agent/src/shared/ui-catalog-prompt.ts owns hidden LLM UI vocabulary outside packages/ui
plugin-coding-tools edit/search/list still need fs Remote parity decisions before additional routing

deletionDeprecationCandidates.reason: Execution-heavy local inference mirrors do not belong in a rendering library once shared/app-core sources cover the same contracts.
validationNeeded: Confirm all consumers can import from packages/shared or app-core API clients
Run app/app-core local inference UI smoke

deletionDeprecationCandidates.reason: The production route shell is app composition, not a reusable UI primitive.
validationNeeded: Keep public @elizaos/ui API compatibility or update packages/app imports
Run packages/app and packaged Electrobun smoke

deletionDeprecationCandidates.reason: Many files are full app pages. Reusable renderers should be split from product routes before moving anything.
validationNeeded: Run packages/app UI and dynamic view tests

ownerDecisionItems: Whether packages/ui should keep exporting App or whether packages/app should own the product shell directly.
How aggressively to shrink packages/ui/src/services/local-inference to shared type/catalog helpers.
Whether app-core desktop renderer roots should remain in app-core or move into packages/app with Electrobun-only adapters.
How to align packages/agent UI spec catalog with packages/ui primitives without coupling agent runtime to React.
Whether browser microphone/playback helpers stay in packages/ui as browser adapters or move under a voice client package.
Whether eliza.fs should add explicit list ignore, ripgrep-style search, glob, and edit/patch methods before the remaining plugin-coding-tools FILE paths route through it.
